page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon processor with 12 vCPUs and 6 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture. The dedicated CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz and includes 38 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 85 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.08 GB of RAM per core.

The server features a bundled NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40 GB of dedicated VRAM. There is no local storage included with this instance type, and it does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff. Single-core CPU performance is weak, scoring in the bottom 25% on stress-ng and Geekbench, with single-threaded gzip compression falling into the bottom 10%. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average range. However, the integrated NVIDIA A100 GPU delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference, specifically for prompt processing and text generation on small 135M and medium 7B models.

Given the high-density GPU and memory allocation relative to the modest 12-vCPU count, this server offers a specialized cost-efficiency profile. It is designed to minimize CPU overhead while maximizing GPU-accelerated throughput. The instance is highly suited for machine learning workloads, LLM inference, data science tasks, and GPU-accelerated compute jobs, whereas it is less optimal for CPU-bound single-threaded applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized virtual machine designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 vCPUs, 6 physical cores, and 85 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes a bundled NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40 GB of VRAM, while local storage is not included. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, and multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth metrics are average. However, the server delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM inference on small and medium models. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for machine learning, data science, and GPU-accelerated workloads that do not require heavy local storage or high single-threaded CPU performance.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 85 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40 GB VRAM, designed for machine learning and LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g features 12 vCPUs, 85 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA A100 GPU, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized virtual machine featuring a dedicated graphics processor and high memory capacity for parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU featuring 40 GB of VRAM.
Features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 85 GB of system memory.
Delivers top-tier performance for small and medium LLM inference workloads.
No local storage is included in this instance configuration.
Suited for machine learning, data science, and GPU-accelerated computing.
